name: Proven
description: Proven, in some interpretations of Slavic mythology, is a deity associated with justice, law, and oaths. While his existence as a widely recognized or universally accepted deity is debated among scholars, the concept of a god embodying truth and righteousness resonates within the broader Slavic cultural context. Some researchers suggest Proven may be a local or regional deity, or perhaps an aspect of a more prominent god like Perun, adapted to emphasize the importance of upholding agreements and seeking fair judgments. His name itself implies 'proven' or 'verified,' suggesting a divine power concerned with ascertaining truth and ensuring accountability. He may have been invoked in legal proceedings, with oaths sworn in his name to guarantee honesty. Those who broke their promises or committed perjury might have feared Proven's wrath, believing he would bring misfortune or divine retribution upon them. Altars or sacred groves dedicated to Proven might have served as places for settling disputes and seeking divine guidance in matters of law and morality. While concrete archaeological evidence for Proven's worship remains elusive, the underlying values he represents – justice, truth, and fidelity – are deeply ingrained in Slavic folklore and traditions. His existence, even if debated, points to the inherent human need for a higher power that upholds ethical principles and ensures fairness within society. The strength of a community often relied on the integrity of its members, and Proven, as a potential embodiment of these values, would have played a crucial role in maintaining social order.
subtitle: God of Justice, Truth, and Oaths

keyMyths.title: The Broken Oath
keyMyths.description: A man breaks an oath sworn to Proven and suffers a series of misfortunes, highlighting the consequences of dishonesty. Eventually, he repents and seeks forgiveness, restoring balance to his life.

keyMyths.title: The Dispute Settled
keyMyths.description: Two villages are locked in a bitter dispute over land. They invoke Proven to guide their decision, and a wise elder interprets a sign to reach a fair compromise.

keyMyths.title: The False Accusation
keyMyths.description: An innocent person is falsely accused of a crime. Through a trial by ordeal, guided by Proven's intervention, the truth is revealed, and justice prevails.

sources.author: Zbigniew Gołąb
sources.description: Discusses the complexities of reconstructing Slavic religious beliefs.
